summary: AGENTS.md is associated with faster runs and lower output-token use in this paired study, but the paper does not establish a causal mechanism and the result is bounded more strongly as an efficiency finding than a correctness finding.

bounded_claims.claim_text: In this paired study of 10 repositories and 124 pull requests, adding AGENTS.md was associated with a statistically significant reduction in median wall-clock runtime.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence shows a statistically significant association within the study setup, but it does not identify the causal pathway by which AGENTS.md reduced runtime; the result is also bounded to the filtered repository set and small merged PR tasks used in the experiment.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Median wall-clock time fell from 98.57 seconds without AGENTS.md to 70.34 seconds with AGENTS.md, a reported reduction of 28.64%.
bounded_claims.bounds: This is a descriptive measured difference in the reported experiment, not a demonstrated explanation of why the reduction occurred, and it should not be generalized beyond the tested repositories and constrained PR selection.

bounded_claims.claim_text: AGENTS.md was also associated with a statistically significant reduction in median output token consumption, from 2,925.00 to 2,440.00 tokens, or 16.58%.
bounded_claims.bounds: The paper reports lower output-token use but does not isolate which properties of AGENTS.md changed agent behavior, so the mechanism remains uncertain; the study also does not show that lower token use preserved full functional equivalence.

bounded_claims.claim_text: These performance findings are bounded by heavy sample filtering and incomplete quality validation, so they support efficiency claims more strongly than correctness claims.
bounded_claims.bounds: The study was narrowed through root-only AGENTS.md selection, additional content filtering and manual verification, small code-change PR constraints, and some generated task statements, while correctness was not fully evaluated; these design choices limit generalization and leave open whether the efficiency gains reliably preserve output quality.
